title: Keyword Search with tsvector

story.i_want_to: search knowledge articles by keyword with relevance ranking
story.so_that: I can quickly find relevant patterns and conventions using natural language terms

rationale: Keyword search is the foundation for all knowledge retrieval. Agents need fast, ranked results when looking for conventions, patterns, or past decisions. PostgreSQL tsvector with GIN indexing provides production-grade full-text search without external dependencies. Weighted vectors (title=A, body=B) ensure title matches rank higher than body matches, which matches how agents scan results.

acceptance_criteria.description: A migration adds a generated tsvector column `search_vector` to the articles table, computed as setweight(to_tsvector('english', coalesce(title, '')), 'A') || setweight(to_tsvector('english', coalesce(body, '')), 'B') so title matches rank higher than body matches.
A GIN index is created on the `search_vector` column for fast full-text search lookups.

The search_vector column is automatically maintained by PostgreSQL as a generated column (GENERATED ALWAYS AS ... STORED), so it updates whenever title or body changes without application-level triggers.

`Loopctl.Knowledge.search_keyword/3` accepts `(tenant_id, query_string, opts)` and returns `{:ok, %{results: [...], meta: %{...}}}`. Uses `websearch_to_tsquery` for the query string (supports quoted phrases, OR, - negation).
Results are ranked using `ts_rank_cd(search_vector, query)` and ordered by rank descending. Each result includes a `relevance_score` field with the rank value.
Each result includes a `snippet` field generated by `ts_headline('english', body, query, 'StartSel=**, StopSel=**, MaxWords=35, MinWords=15')` highlighting matching terms.
Results are filterable via opts: `:project_id` (exact match), `:category` (exact match), `:tags` (array overlap), `:status` (default: `:published` only). All filters combine with AND.
Results are paginated with limit/offset: `opts[:limit]` defaults to 20, max 100; `opts[:offset]` defaults to 0. Response meta includes `total_count`, `limit`, and `offset`.
Empty query string returns `{:error, :empty_query}`. Query strings that produce empty tsquery (all stop words) return an empty result set, not an error.
All queries are scoped to the current tenant via both application-level WHERE clause and RLS policy on the articles table.

The q parameter is validated to max 500 characters before being passed to websearch_to_tsquery. Returns {:error, :bad_request, "Query too long (max 500 characters)"} if exceeded. Empty tsquery results (e.g., stop-word-only queries) short-circuit to empty results.

test_cases.name: keyword search returns ranked results with snippets
preconditions: fixture(:tenant) as tenant
fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, title: 'Elixir Pattern Matching', body: 'Pattern matching is fundamental to Elixir...', status: :published})
fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, title: 'Phoenix LiveView', body: 'LiveView uses pattern matching in handle_event...', status: :published})
fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, title: 'Deployment Guide', body: 'Deploy to Fly.io using mix release...', status: :published})
expected_results: Returns {:ok, result} with 2 articles matching 'pattern matching'
The 'Elixir Pattern Matching' article ranks higher (title=weight A)
Each result includes a relevance_score > 0
Each result includes a snippet with highlighted terms
'Deployment Guide' is not in results

test_cases.name: search filters by project_id, category, and tags
preconditions: fixture(:tenant) as tenant
fixture(:project, %{tenant_id: tenant.id}) as project_a
fixture(:project, %{tenant_id: tenant.id}) as project_b
fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project_a.id, category: :convention, tags: ['elixir'], title: 'Elixir Conventions', body: 'Use pipe operator...', status: :published})
fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project_b.id, category: :reference, tags: ['docker'], title: 'Elixir Deployment', body: 'Use Docker for Elixir deploys...', status: :published})
expected_results: project_id filter returns only the project_a article
category filter returns only the :convention article
tags filter returns only the :reference article with 'docker' tag

test_cases.name: search defaults to published-only articles
preconditions: fixture(:tenant) as tenant
fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, title: 'Published Guide', body: 'Testing conventions...', status: :published})
fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, title: 'Draft Guide', body: 'Testing conventions draft...', status: :draft})
expected_results: Default search returns only the published article
Explicit status: :draft returns only the draft article

test_cases.name: pagination with limit and offset
preconditions: fixture(:tenant) as tenant
Create 5 published articles with 'testing' in the body
expected_results: First call returns 2 results with meta.total_count=5
Second call returns 2 different results with meta.total_count=5
Results are ordered by relevance_score descending

test_cases.name: empty and stop-word-only queries handled correctly
preconditions: fixture(:tenant) as tenant
expected_results: Empty string returns {:error, :empty_query}
Stop-word-only query returns {:ok, %{results: [], meta: %{total_count: 0, ...}}}

test_cases.name: tenant isolation in keyword search
preconditions: fixture(:tenant) as tenant_a
fixture(:tenant) as tenant_b
fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant_a.id, title: 'Secret Pattern', body: 'Tenant A secret...', status: :published})
fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant_b.id, title: 'Public Pattern', body: 'Tenant B public...', status: :published})
expected_results: Only tenant_b's article is returned
Tenant A's 'Secret Pattern' is not visible to tenant B

test_cases.name: search_vector updates when article title or body changes
preconditions: fixture(:tenant) as tenant
fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, title: 'Old Title', body: 'Old content about deployment...', status: :published})
steps: Update the article title to 'New Title' and body to 'New content about testing...'
Call Knowledge.search_keyword(tenant.id, 'deployment', %{}) -- should NOT find it
expected_results: Initial search for 'deployment' finds the article
After update, 'deployment' no longer matches
After update, 'testing' now matches
Generated column automatically reflects the updated content

technical_notes: Migration adds: `execute "ALTER TABLE articles ADD COLUMN search_vector tsvector GENERATED ALWAYS AS (setweight(to_tsvector('english', coalesce(title, '')), 'A') || setweight(to_tsvector('english', coalesce(body, '')), 'B')) STORED"` — this is a PostgreSQL generated column, not an Ecto-managed field.
GIN index: `CREATE INDEX articles_search_vector_idx ON articles USING GIN (search_vector)`.
Use `websearch_to_tsquery('english', query)` rather than `plainto_tsquery` because it supports quoted phrases ('"exact phrase"'), OR operators, and negation (-term) out of the box.
Ranking: `ts_rank_cd(search_vector, query)` uses cover density ranking which handles proximity better than `ts_rank`.
Snippet generation: `ts_headline('english', body, query, 'StartSel=**, StopSel=**, MaxWords=35, MinWords=15')` — use ** delimiters for markdown-friendly bold highlighting.
The search_vector column should NOT be in the Ecto schema (it's a generated column). Use `Ecto.Query.select_merge` or raw fragment to include rank and snippet in results.
Opts validation: limit clamped to 1..100, offset must be >= 0. Invalid values return {:error, changeset}.

Testing infrastructure: (1) All tests use async: true via DataCase/ConnCase, (2) Mox mocks defined in test/support/mocks.ex, (3) Default permissive stubs set in DataCase/ConnCase setup via stub_all_defaults/0 and Mox.set_mox_from_context(tags) for async isolation, (4) setup :verify_on_exit! in every test module, (5) Fixtures in test/support/fixtures.ex with build/2 (no DB) and fixture/2 (DB insert with auto-dependency resolution), (6) NEVER Application.put_env in tests — all service swapping via config/test.exs.

The Article schema (from US-19.1) uses use Loopctl.Schema with tenant_field(), binary IDs, and microsecond timestamps. No schema changes needed for this story — only the migration adds the generated tsvector column.
